title: चण्डी हृदयम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री चण्डी हृदयम् ॥

ॐ अस्य श्रीचण्डीहृदयस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य भार्गव ऋषिः।
अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीचण्डिका देवता।
श्रीचण्डिकाप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

ॐ नमश्चण्डिकायै॥

प्रथमं कवचं देव्या द्वितीयं किलकं तथा।
अर्गलास्तोत्रमेवाथ चण्डिकाहृदयं परम्॥१॥

एतान्यङ्गानि देव्यास्तु सप्तशत्याः प्रकीर्तिताः।
चतुर्भिः सिद्धिमाप्नोति ततः प्रारभ्य पाठनम्॥२॥

चण्डिकाहृदयं ज्ञात्वा यो जपेत् सततं नरः।
तस्य सर्वभयं नश्येत् सर्वसम्पत्तिरायुषा॥३॥

ॐ ह्रीं चण्डिकायै हृदयाय नमः।
ॐ ह्रीं चण्डिकायै शिरसे स्वाहा।
ॐ ह्रीं चण्डिकायै शिखायै वषट्।
ॐ ह्रीं चण्डिकायै कवचाय हुम्।
ॐ ह्रीं चण्डिकायै नेत्रत्रयाय वौषट्।
ॐ ह्रीं चण्डिकायै अस्त्राय फट्॥४॥

त्रयाणामपि लोकानां माता त्वं सर्वदेहिनाम्।
महाविद्ये महामाये जय त्वं चण्डिके नमः॥५॥

कामेश्वरी कामसिद्धिं लभते चण्डिकास्मृतेः।
सर्वकामप्रदे देवि सर्वतो जयमन्विताम्॥६॥

त्वमेव विश्वजननी त्वमेव विश्वपालिनी।
त्वमेव विश्वहन्त्री च त्वमेव सर्वरूपिणी॥७॥

सृष्टिसंहारकर्त्री च तथा स्थितिविधायिनी।
महामाये महाशक्ते चण्डिके सर्वमंगले॥८॥

महालक्ष्मी महाकाली महासरस्वती शिवा।
तिस्रो देव्यस्त्रयो भागा मूर्तिभेदेन संस्थिताः॥९॥

त्रिगुणात्मिका देवी त्रिविधां शक्तिमुद्वहत्।
सत्त्वं रजस्तमश्चेति गुणत्रयविभागिनी॥१०॥

दुर्गा सती भद्रकाली चण्डी सिंहवाहिनी।
बगला वैष्णवी माता शाकम्भरी शिवप्रिया॥११॥

सर्वमन्त्रमयी विद्या सर्वतन्त्रेषु गोपिता।
सर्वयन्त्रात्मिका शक्तिः सर्वयन्त्रनिवासिनी॥१२॥

यो जपेत् सततं भक्त्या चण्डिकाहृदयं शुभम्।
सर्वसिद्धिमवाप्नोति नात्र कार्या विचारणा॥१३॥

गोपनीयं प्रयत्नेन स्वयोनिरिव पार्वति।
मारणोच्चाटने वश्ये स्तम्भनाकर्षणादिषु॥१४॥

सर्वक्रियासु सर्वत्र हृदयस्य जपं चरेत्।
इदं रहस्यं परमं देव्याः चण्डीहृदयकम्॥१५॥

॥ इति श्री चण्डीहृदयं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Chandi Hridayam ||

Om Asya Shri Chandi-hridaya-stotra-mantrasya Bhargava Rishiph.
Anushtup Chhandah. Shri Chandika Devata.
Shri Chandika-prityarthe Jape Viniyogah.

Om Namashchandikayai.

Prathamam Kavacham Devya Dvitiyam Kilakam Tatha.
Argala-stotram Evatha Chandika-hridayam Param.||1||

Etany Angani Devyas Tu Saptashatyah Prakirtitah.
Chaturbhih Siddhim Apnoti Tatah Prarabhya Pathanam.||2||

Chandika-hridayam Jnatva Yo Japet Satatam Narah.
Tasya Sarva-bhayam Nashyet Sarva-sampatir Ayusha.||3||

Om Hrim Chandikayai Hridayaya Namah.
Om Hrim Chandikayai Shirase Svaha.
Om Hrim Chandikayai Shikhayai Vashat.
Om Hrim Chandikayai Kavachaya Hum.
Om Hrim Chandikayai Netra-trayaya Vaushat.
Om Hrim Chandikayai Astraya Phat.||4||

Trayanam Api Lokanam Mata Tvam Sarva-dehinam.
Mahavidye Mahamaye Jaya Tvam Chandike Namah.||5||

Kameshvari Kama-siddhim Labhate Chandika-smriteh.
Sarva-kama-prade Devi Sarvato Jayam Anvitam.||6||

Tvam Eva Vishva-janani Tvam Eva Vishva-palini.
Tvam Eva Vishva-hantri Cha Tvam Eva Sarva-rupini.||7||

Srishti-samhara-kartri Cha Tatha Sthiti-vidhayini.
Mahamaye Mahashakte Chandike Sarva-mangale.||8||

Mahalakshmi Mahakali Mahasarasvati Shiva.
Tisro Devyas Trayo Bhaga Murti-bhedena Samsthitah.||9||

Tri-gunatmika Devi Tri-vidham Shaktim Udvahat.
Sattvam Rajas Tamash Cheti Guna-traya-vibhagini.||10||

Durga Sati Bhadrakali Chandi Simha-vahini.
Bagala Vaishnavi Mata Shakambhari Shiva-priya.||11||

Sarva-mantra-mayi Vidya Sarva-tantreshu Gopita.
Sarva-yantratmika Shaktih Sarva-yantra-nivasini.||12||

Yo Japet Satatam Bhaktya Chandika-hridayam Shubham.
Sarva-siddhim Avapnoti Natra Karya Vicharana.||13||

Gopaniyam Prayatnena Sva-yonir Iva Parvati.
Marano-chchatane Vashye Stambhana-akarshana-adishu.||14||

Sarva-kriyasu Sarvatra Hridayasya Japam Charet.
Idam Rahasyam Paramam Devyah Chandi-hridayakam.||15||

|| Iti Shri Chandi-hridayam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
देवी के कवच, कीलक, अर्गलास्तोत्र और चण्डीहृदय - ये चार अंग सप्तशती के कहे गए हैं। इन चारों सहित पाठ करने से सिद्धि प्राप्त होती है।

जो मनुष्य चण्डिकाहृदय को जानकर निरन्तर जप करता है, उसका सब भय नष्ट होता है और सर्वसम्पत्ति एवं आयु की प्राप्ति होती है।

हे देवी! तुम तीनों लोकों की और समस्त देहधारियों की माता हो। महाविद्या, महामाया! चण्डिके! जय हो, नमस्कार।

तुम्हीं विश्व की जननी, पालिनी और संहारकर्त्री हो। तुम सर्वरूपिणी हो। महालक्ष्मी, महाकाली, महासरस्वती - तीन देवियाँ तीन भागों में मूर्तिभेद से स्थित हैं।

त्रिगुणात्मिका देवी सत्त्व-रज-तम तीन शक्तियों को धारण करती हैं। दुर्गा, सती, भद्रकाली, चण्डी, बगला, वैष्णवी, शाकम्भरी - सब उन्हीं के रूप हैं।

जो भक्तिपूर्वक इस हृदय का जप करता है, वह सर्वसिद्धि प्राप्त करता है। यह परम रहस्य है, गोपनीय रखना चाहिए।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीचण्डीहृदयस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य भार्गव ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीचण्डिका देवता। ॐ ह्रीं बीजम्। चण्डिका शक्तिः। श्रीचण्डिकाप्रीत्यर्थे चण्डीहृदयपाठे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Reveals the innermost heart-essence of Chandika Devi worship
Essential fourth anga (limb) of complete Durga Saptashati recitation
Traditionally recited for removal of all fears and grants long life with full prosperity
Invokes the Triguna (Sattva-Rajas-Tamas) form of the supreme Goddess
Invokes the blessing of all siddhis without doubt when recited with devotion
Powerful for all tantric applications including protection and attraction
description: Dedicated to Goddess Durga, the Chandi Hridayam (चण्डी हृदयम्) is a heart-essence prayer revered in Hindu tradition. Drawn from Markandeya Purana (Chandi Path), this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Devotees recite this prayer to destroy all fears and grant long life with full prosperity and bestow all siddhis without doubt when recited with devotion. It is also said to invoke the Triguna (Sattva-Rajas-Tamas) form of the supreme Goddess. For best results, devotees chant this on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri. At 15 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 10 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Chandi Hridayam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Chandi Hridayam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. The heart-essence of Chandika worship, essential anga of Durga Saptashati for complete siddhis.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Chandi Hridayam and where is it found?
seoFaqs.answer: The Chandi Hridayam is one of the angas (subsidiary limbs) of the Durga Saptashati or Chandi Path, the 700-verse Devi Mahatmyam embedded in the Markandeya Purana. The full traditional Chandi Path recitation is preceded by six angas — Kavach, Argala, Keelaka, Vedoktam Ratri Suktam, Tantroktam Ratri Suktam — and the Hridayam is recited as the heart-essence of the entire text, distilling its spiritual core into a compact stotra. Without first reciting the angas, classical pundits hold that the Chandi Path itself does not yield its full phala. (Sources: Hindupedia Devi Mahatmyam; Stotra Nidhi; Sanatan Sanstha.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is it called Hridayam (heart)?
seoFaqs.answer: In Sanskrit stotra terminology, a Hridayam is the heart-essence text that captures the inner meaning of a longer scripture or the inmost prayer of a deity. The Chandi Hridayam is so called because it is regarded as the very heart of Goddess Chandika herself, the prayer that she most wishes her devotees to know. It identifies her as the supreme Adi-Shakti from whom Brahma, Vishnu and Shiva derive their own powers, and praises her as the unmanifest substratum of all manifestation. Reciting the Hridayam is therefore traditionally said to give direct access to her grace without the elaborate ritual apparatus that ordinarily mediates Devi worship. (Sources: Hindupedia; Stotra Nidhi; Sanskritdocuments.org.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Chandi Hridayam fit into Navaratri Saptashati paath?
seoFaqs.answer: During Navaratri the traditional sequence for serious Saptashati paath is: Achamanam, Pranayama, Sankalpa, Ganesh Smarana, Devi Dhyana, then the six angas (Kavach, Argala, Keelaka, Vedoktam Ratri Suktam, Tantroktam Ratri Suktam, Hridayam), then the thirteen chapters of the Saptashati, and finally concluding rites including Aparadha-kshamapana. The Hridayam is therefore recited daily as part of the angas before each session of paath. Some devotees who cannot complete the full thirteen chapters recite only the angas plus selected chapters or the Saptashloki Durga, with the Hridayam always retained as essential. (Sources: Sanatan Sanstha Saptashati Vidhi; Hindupedia; Drikpanchang Navratri.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Chandi or Chandika?
seoFaqs.answer: Chandi is the fierce form of Mahadevi who manifests in the Devi Mahatmyam to slay the asuras Madhu, Kaitabha, Mahishasura, Shumbha and Nishumbha. Her name derives from chand, meaning fierce or angry, expressing her righteous wrath against adharma. She is identical with Durga, Ambika and Kaushiki, and in the highest theology of the Devi Mahatmyam she is the supreme Brahman-Shakti from whom Mahalakshmi, Mahasaraswati and Mahakali emerge as her three primary forms. The text declares that all forms of the Goddess across all traditions are ultimately Chandika in essence. (Sources: Devi Mahatmyam; Hindupedia Chandi; Wikipedia Devi Mahatmya.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Chandi Hridayam confer?
seoFaqs.answer: Reciting the Hridayam is said to grant the merit of the entire Chandi Path even when time does not permit the full thirteen chapters, to remove the obstacles of the six inner enemies, to protect from black magic, ghosts, planetary afflictions and untimely death, to bestow victory over external adversaries especially in litigation and conflict, to grant prosperity through Mahalakshmi's grace, wisdom through Mahasaraswati's grace, and fearlessness through Mahakali's grace. Daily recitation during Navaratri is held to amplify the phala manifold. The stotra is also recited at the conclusion of homas and yajnas dedicated to the Devi. (Sources: Hindupedia; Sanatan Sanstha; Stotra Nidhi.)

seoFaqs.question: Is Chandi Hridayam open to all devotees for recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: Yes. As an anga of the Saptashati and a Pauranika praise text, the Hridayam is open to all devotees. The Saptashati itself, however, has more elaborate recitation rules: traditional pundits maintain that for the most rigorous prayoga (anushthana, navaha-paath), male initiates with proper guru lineage are preferred and women refrain during the four days of menstruation. For ordinary daily recitation in household puja, no such restrictions apply, and women, children and elderly devotees commonly recite the Hridayam, the Saptashloki Durga or the Argala-Keelaka-Hridayam triad as a complete short Devi worship. (Sources: Sanatan Sanstha Saptashati Vidhi; Hindupedia; Drikpanchang.)
